question: A company claims their new algorithm sorts a list in an average of 150 milliseconds. To test this, you run the algorithm 100 times and record a sample mean of 153 milliseconds with a standard deviation of 10 milliseconds. Formulate the null and alternative hypotheses and calculate the z-score for this test. What does this z-score represent?
expectedPoints: Interpretation: The observed sample mean is 3 standard errors above the hypothesized population mean.

question: Explain why the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) is a critical prerequisite for conducting a large sample test of significance for a population mean. What does it allow us to assume about the sampling distribution?
expectedPoints: The CLT states that for a large sample size (n > 30), the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normally distributed.
This holds true regardless of the shape of the underlying population distribution.
This normality allows us to use the standard normal (z) distribution to find critical values and p-values for our hypothesis test.
It justifies the use of the formula for the standard error: σ/√n, even if the population standard deviation σ is unknown and we use the sample standard deviation s as an estimate.

question: In a large sample z-test for a proportion, what is the formula for the test statistic?
expectedPoints: Where p̂ is the sample proportion.
p₀ is the hypothesized population proportion under the null hypothesis.
